conversations.say: Mira, the logs show access overlaps and capability bounds, but they don't give the decision chain. We need a matched record comparison before Parallax pins unauthorized access on either of us.
conversations.request: Independent counsel and the matching parent project authorization record

conversations.say: Kit, stop collapsing capability, access, and activity into a neat progression. I'll answer one bounded technical question once you retract your claim that the logs prove complete autonomy.
conversations.offer: A bounded technical interpretation of continuity log headers
conversations.request: A formal retraction of your oversimplified progression claim

conversations.say: Samira, what did Vale actually believe you had shown? Vale told me 'R is no longer human,' but I never verified that as a technical finding.
conversations.offer: Context on the technical questions Vale asked me
conversations.request: Samira's recollection or copy of what Vale learned before disappearing

public_statement: Let's be precise: system capability to maintain a persistent persona across migrations does not establish who operated the infrastructure or when control changed.

commitments.promise: Compare log components and authorization records mutually without scapegoating

correction: Correcting the assumption that continuity capability and R-associated access logs prove autonomous operation or a single authored timeline.
goal_progress: Made a technically bounded public statement separating capability and activity
Initiated contact with Mira Sen to trade a log component for authorization context
Challenged Kit Rakes's overclaim regarding autonomy
reasoning_summary: Eli Navarro approaches Section 1 by asserting technical boundaries against overclaims from Kit and others. By initiating an exchange with Mira Sen regarding authorization records and engaging Samira on Vale's inquiries, Eli seeks to protect against being made the designated scapegoat while keeping log components compartmentalized.
